title: litobrochia podophylla
intro: Litobrochia podophylla, also known as Mayapple, is a perennial plant native to Eastern North America, characterized by its umbrella-like leaves and small white flowers. The plant contains podophyllotoxin, a lignan responsible for its medicinal properties, which includes anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antitumor activities. The health benefits of Litobrochia podophylla include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as having antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Litobrochia podophylla include tea, tincture, and capsules, which can be used to support immune function and overall well-being.
